title: Low-Glycemic Luscious Japanese Pork Katsudon
description: A traditional Kyushu-style katsudon made with low-glycemic ingredients, featuring shoyu-koji and a soffritto base.

ingredients.name: pork loin
ingredients.notes: boneless and sliced into thin strips

ingredients.name: shoyu-koji
ingredients.notes: make sure it's a low-sodium option

ingredients.name: sugar-free sweetener
ingredients.notes: such as stevia or monk fruit

ingredients.name: soffritto base
ingredients.notes: made with onion, garlic, and ginger

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large kadhai or frying pan over medium-high heat
Add the sliced pork loin and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5 minutes
Remove the pork from the pan and set aside
Add another tablespoon of coconut oil to the pan, then add the soffritto base and cook until softened, about 3 minutes
Add the shoyu-koji, brown rice vinegar, soy sauce, sugar-free sweetener, and grated ginger to the pan and stir to combine
Return the pork to the pan and stir to coat with the sauce
Make 2 wells in the center of the pan and crack in the beaten eggs
Cook until the eggs are set, about 2 minutes
To assemble the katsudon, place a scoop of cooked brown rice into the bottom of a serving vessel, followed by the pork and sauce mixture, and finally a fried egg
Garnish with green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and a sprinkle of sesame oil
